step_rationale: Focal adhesion protein mediating integrin activation; upregulated in thrombus platelets; implicated in endothelial erosion pathology.
mechanism: FERMT3 is a focal adhesion protein that mediates platelet integrin activation and firm adhesion, with elevated expression marking plaque erosion and thrombosis.

references.title: Proteomics Profiling Reveals Insulin-Like Growth Factor 1, Collagen Type VI α-2 Chain, and Fermitin Family Homolog 3 as Potential Biomarkers of Plaque Erosion in ST-Segment Elevated Myocardial Infarction.
references.journal: Circulation journal : official journal of the Japanese Circulation Society

references.title: The Integrin Activating Protein Kindlin-3 Is Cleaved in Human Platelets during ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction.
Proteomic characterization of human coronary thrombus in patients with ST-segment elevation acute myocardial infarction.
